target.title: fix(doctor): skip cleanup hints for active gateway and downgrade exec SecretRef errors to warnings
target.body:
## Fixes #52510

### Problem

`openclaw doctor` has two false-positive issues:

1. **Cleanup hints suggest removing the active gateway** - When doctor detects ANY extra gateway-like services (e.g., an unrelated `imessage-watcher`), it unconditionally generates cleanup commands for the main gateway plist. Following these hints kills the running gateway and causes downtime.

2. **SecretRef exit code 1** - Doctor exits with code 1 when encountering `exec:keychain:` SecretRef values in config. These are resolved at gateway runtime and are expected to be unavailable during doctor execution.

### Changes

**Bug 1: `src/daemon/inspect.ts` + `src/commands/doctor-gateway-services.ts`**
- `renderGatewayServiceCleanupHints()` now takes the actual detected extra services as input
- Generates per-service cleanup commands based on what was actually found
- Returns empty array when no services are provided
- Call site passes only non-legacy extra services

**Bug 2: `src/config/types.secrets.ts` + `src/commands/doctor.ts`**
- Doctor sets `OPENCLAW_ALLOW_EXEC_SECRETREF_FAILURES=true` during execution (with proper try/finally cleanup)
- `assertSecretInputResolved()` detects doctor context and downgrades `exec:` SecretRef failures to warnings instead of throwing
- Original throwing behavior preserved for all non-doctor contexts

### Testing

- Existing tests use mocked `renderGatewayServiceCleanupHints`, so signature change is safe
- Manually verified: doctor no longer suggests removing active gateway when only non-gateway extras are detected
- Manually verified: doctor completes with exit 0 when `exec:keychain:` SecretRefs are present

target.title: fix(pdf): time out idle remote PDF reads
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes #68649.
- Bound remote PDF body reads by passing a 120s `readIdleTimeoutMs` from the PDF tool into `loadWebMediaRaw`.
- Thread `readIdleTimeoutMs` through `web-media` into `readRemoteMediaBuffer` so stalled remote PDF downloads fail instead of leaving the session stuck.
- Keep local/sandboxed PDF paths unchanged; the timeout is only applied to http(s) PDF references.

## Behavior proof

Issue #68649 reports a remote PDF tool call that never returns, leaving the session queued behind a stuck tool call. This branch adds regression coverage that a stalling remote raw media body trips the idle timeout, and that an active remote PDF still loads successfully. The PDF tool test also verifies remote PDF loads pass the shared 120s idle timeout while preserving existing SSRF policy plumbing.

## Validation

- `git fetch origin && git rebase origin/main` - success
- `pnpm check:changed --base origin/main` - pass
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD` - pass
- `pnpm check:no-conflict-markers` - pass
- `gitleaks detect --source /code/workspaces/openclaw/openclaw --log-opts origin/main..HEAD --redact --no-banner` - pass, no leaks found

## Scope boundary

This PR does not change `/new` or `/restart` queue bypass behavior. It addresses the narrow stuck remote PDF fetch path by ensuring remote PDF body reads have an idle timeout.

## Summary

Adds [repotype](https://supernalintelligence.github.io/repotype/) for repository structure enforcement — a repo-level linter that complements existing code linters.

## What it does

- **Skills:** Ensures every skill has `SKILL.md`
- **Packages:** Validates `package.json` and `README.md` exist
- **Extensions:** Validates `manifest.json` exists
- **Permissive mode:** Starts with `unmatchedFiles: allow` (can tighten later)

## Why

With 50+ skills and many contributors, structural consistency matters. This catches missing files **before** they land in main.

## Usage

```bash
npx repotype validate .    # Check structure
npx repotype fix .         # Auto-fix issues
```

## CI integration (optional, not included in this PR)

```yaml
- run: npx repotype validate . --json
```

## Links

- [Repotype docs](https://supernalintelligence.github.io/repotype/)
- [OpenClaw example config](https://supernalintelligence.github.io/repotype/examples/openclaw-project/)

---

Built to Help Fight Entropy with ♥ from [Supernal Intelligence](https://supernal.ai)

target.title: fix(windows): PowerShell completion install and time-format detection
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** On Windows, `openclaw completion --install --shell powershell` silently printed "not supported" and exited without writing anything. Additionally, the time-format detection always invoked PS 5.1 (`powershell.exe`) even when PS7 (`pwsh.exe`) was installed.
- **Why it matters:** PowerShell is the default shell on Windows — completion install was completely broken for the platform, and PS5.1 lacks features and is deprecated on modern Windows.
- **What changed:** Added the missing `powershell` branch in `installCompletion()`, corrected the source-line syntax from bash `source` to PS dot-source (`. `), and switched `detectSystemTimeFormat()` to use `resolvePowerShellPath()` (which already prefers PS7).
- **What did NOT change:** No changes to completion script generation, shell detection logic, or any non-Windows code paths.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix

## Scope

- [x] UI / DX

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes # *(no existing issue — discovered while using OpenClaw on Windows)*

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- `openclaw completion --install --shell powershell` now works on Windows: creates/updates `Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1` with `. "${cachePath}"` dot-source syntax.
- On Windows with PS7 installed, time-format detection now correctly invokes `pwsh.exe` instead of `powershell.exe`.

## Security Impact

- New permissions/capabilities? No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No — `resolvePowerShellPath()` was already used throughout `shell-utils.ts`; this aligns `date-time.ts` with the existing pattern.
- Data access scope changed? No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Windows 11 (x64), Node v24.7.0
- Runtime/container: Native Windows (no WSL)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: N/A
- Relevant config: N/A

### Steps

1. Install OpenClaw on Windows with PowerShell 5.1 as default shell
2. Run `openclaw completion --write-state`
3. Run `openclaw completion --install --shell powershell`

### Expected

- Profile at `%USERPROFILE%\Documents\PowerShell\Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1` is created/updated with `. "${cachePath}"`

### Actual (before fix)

- Prints `Automated installation not supported for powershell yet.` and exits with no file written

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

```
# Before fix: installCompletion("powershell") hit the else-branch silently
# After fix: 7/7 tests pass

✓ detects pwsh from SHELL env
✓ detects powershell from SHELL env basename
✓ returns zsh as default when SHELL is unset
✓ cache path for powershell uses .ps1 extension
✓ installs PowerShell completion with dot-source syntax
✓ detects completion as installed after writing profile
✓ does not use bash source keyword in PowerShell profile

Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests       7 passed (7)
```

## Human Verification

- **Verified scenarios:** TypeScript typecheck (`tsc --noEmit`) clean, oxlint 0 errors, oxfmt clean, all 7 new tests pass on Windows.
- **Edge cases checked:** Profile already exists (idempotent update), profile directory doesn't exist (auto-created), `isCompletionInstalled()` correctly detects after install.
- **What I did not verify:** Actual runtime execution on macOS/Linux (no code path changes for those platforms); PS7 time-format path requires a Windows machine with pwsh.exe installed.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Failure Recovery

- How to revert: `git revert` the single commit; no config changes were made.
- Known bad symptoms: None expected — the PS branch was entirely missing before, so any regression would be a no-op (same "not supported" behaviour as before).

## Risks and Mitigations

- **Risk:** Profile path for PowerShell on macOS/Linux (`.config/powershell/Microsoft.PowerShell_profile.ps1`) — untested on those platforms with `pwsh` installed.
  - **Mitigation:** Path logic was already present in `getShellProfilePath()` and is unchanged; this PR only wires up the call that was missing.

target.title: feat(web-fetch): add allowPrivateNetwork config for web_fetch
target.body:
## Summary

- Add opt-in `tools.web.fetch.allowPrivateNetwork` config key (boolean, default `false`) so `web_fetch` can reach private/internal network addresses (localhost, 10.x, 192.168.x, 172.16-31.x) when explicitly enabled.
- Default behavior is unchanged: private networks remain blocked unless the operator explicitly opts in.

## Problem

`web_fetch` blocks all private/internal network addresses via the SSRF guard. There is no config-level way to opt in to private network access, blocking agent architectures where agents need to call local services:

```
[security] blocked URL fetch (url-fetch) target=http://127.0.0.1:9090/api/...
reason=Blocked hostname or private/internal/special-use IP address
```

The `ToolsWebFetchSchema` uses `.strict()`, so users cannot work around this without an upstream schema change.

## Changes

- **`src/infra/net/ssrf.ts`**: Add optional `options.allowPrivateNetwork` parameter to `resolvePinnedHostname()`. When `true`, skips blocked-hostname and private-IP checks; pins private IP literals directly without DNS lookup.
- **`src/agents/tools/web-fetch.ts`**: Add `resolveFetchAllowPrivateNetwork()` resolver (defaults `false`). Thread `allowPrivateNetwork` through `fetchWithRedirects` and `runWebFetch` into `resolvePinnedHostname`.
- **`src/config/zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts`**: Add `allowPrivateNetwork: z.boolean().optional()` to `ToolsWebFetchSchema`.
- **`src/config/types.tools.ts`**: Add `allowPrivateNetwork?: boolean` to the fetch config type.
- **`src/config/schema.ts`**: Add label and help text for the new config key.
- **`src/agents/tools/web-fetch.ssrf.test.ts`**: Add two tests (allow private when enabled; block private when explicitly false). Fix mock to forward the new `options` parameter.
- **`CHANGELOG.md`**: Add entry referencing #39604.

## User-visible changes

New config key:
```json5
"tools": {
  "web": {
    "fetch": { "allowPrivateNetwork": true }
  }
}
```
When `true`, `web_fetch` can reach private/internal network addresses. Default remains `false` (no behavior change for existing users).

## Security impact

- **New capability**: opt-in only, default `false`.
- **Risk**: operators enabling this in untrusted environments could expose internal services.
- **Mitigation**: explicit config required; secure default; same trust model as operator-controlled gateway config.

## Testing performed

- `pnpm lint` -- 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `pnpm build` -- clean
- `pnpm test` -- 5947 passed, 2 failed (pre-existing lobster subprocess timeout, unrelated)
- SSRF test suite: 7/7 passed (including 2 new tests for the `allowPrivateNetwork` path)

Closes #39604

target.title: fix: reload config in slash command routing so dmScope is respected
target.body:
Fixes #39605

## Problem

Native slash commands (`/status`, `/ask`, etc.) in Discord, Telegram, and Slack DMs route to `agent:main:main` instead of respecting `session.dmScope` (e.g. `per-channel-peer`). Normal text messages in the same conversation correctly route to the expected session key like `agent:main:discord:direct:<userId>`.

## Root Cause

All three channel providers load config once at startup and capture it in a closure. The message handlers already call `loadConfig()` fresh per message (e.g. `message-handler.preflight.ts`), but the slash/native-command routing paths reuse the stale startup snapshot. Since `resolveAgentRoute` reads `cfg.session.dmScope` to compute the session key, slash commands always use the config state from startup time.

## Fix

Call `loadConfig()` in each channel's slash/native-command routing section before route resolution, matching the pattern already used by message handlers.

### Changes

- **`src/discord/monitor/native-command.ts`**: Load fresh config in `dispatchDiscordCommandInteraction` before routing; promote `loadConfig` from type-only to value import
- **`src/telegram/bot-native-commands.ts`**: Load fresh config in `resolveCommandRuntimeContext` before routing; add `loadConfig` value import
- **`src/slack/monitor/slash.ts`**: Load fresh config in `handleSlashCommand` before routing; add `loadConfig` import

### Testing

- Type-check clean (`pnpm tsgo`)
- Lint clean (`pnpm lint` — only pre-existing `debug-route.ts` error)
- All routing tests pass (`src/routing/resolve-route.test.ts`, `src/routing/session-key.test.ts`)

target.title: config: Support setting userTimezone per agent
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: OpenClaw only supported a single global `agents.defaults.userTimezone`, so multi-agent setups could not give different agents correct local time context.
- Why it matters: Agent-local time affects system prompt time context, heartbeat active-hours, session status, envelope timestamps, system event timestamps, and time-based helper prompts like memory flush/post-compaction context.
- What changed: Added `agents.list[].userTimezone`, introduced shared agent-aware timezone resolution, threaded `agentId` through the relevant call sites, validated configured IANA timezones in schema, and added tests/docs for the new behavior.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): This does not change `timeFormat` per agent, host timezone fallback behavior, or explicit non-`user` timezone settings such as fixed IANA/UTC/local envelope config.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [x] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #39579 

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- New optional config: `agents.list[].userTimezone`.
- When set, the active agent's `userTimezone` now overrides `agents.defaults.userTimezone`.
- This per-agent timezone is now used for:
  - system prompt time context
  - `session_status` time line
  - heartbeat `activeHours.timezone: "user"`
  - `"user"` envelope timestamps
  - `"user"` system-event timestamps
  - cron-style current-time injection used by memory flush and post-compaction context
- If no per-agent value is set, behavior remains: `agents.defaults.userTimezone` then host timezone fallback.
- Config validation now rejects invalid IANA timezone strings for both default and per-agent `userTimezone`.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation:

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux
- Runtime/container: podman
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): gateway + multi-agent config; verified via user testing
- Relevant config (redacted):
  ```json5
  {
    agents: {
      defaults: {
        userTimezone: "America/New_York",
        envelopeTimezone: "user",
      },
      list: [
        {
          id: "work",
          userTimezone: "America/Los_Angeles",
        },
      ],
    },
  }
  ```

### Steps

1. Configure `agents.defaults.userTimezone` and a different `agents.list[].userTimezone` for a non-default agent.
2. Run flows using that agent, including session status, prompt/time injection paths, and any envelope/system-event path using `"user"` timezone.
3. Observe the active agent uses its own timezone instead of the global default.

### Expected

- The active agent consistently uses its own `userTimezone` override everywhere `"user"` timezone semantics are intended.

### Actual

- Before this change, the default/global timezone was used even for non-default agents.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [ ] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios:
  - Multi-agent config with different default/per-agent timezones
  - User-visible behavior manually tested as a user
  - Full local validation: `pnpm build && pnpm check && pnpm test`
- Edge cases checked:
  - Agent without override falls back to `agents.defaults.userTimezone`
  - No configured timezone falls back to host timezone
  - Invalid configured timezones are rejected by schema validation
  - Envelope/system-event `"user"` timezone paths receive the active `agentId`
- What you did **not** verify:
  - Every external channel manually end-to-end in a live environment

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps:
  - Optional only: add `agents.list[].userTimezone` where agent-specific timezone behavior is desired.

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- How to disable/revert this change quickly:
  - Remove per-agent `userTimezone` entries and rely on `agents.defaults.userTimezone` or revert this PR.
- Files/config to restore:
  - Agent timezone config in `agents.list[]`
- Known bad symptoms reviewers should watch for:
  - Non-default agents showing the default timezone in prompts, envelopes, heartbeat windows, or status output
  - `"user"` timezone behavior not varying by agent

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A call site using `"user"` timezone semantics could miss passing `agentId` and silently fall back to the default timezone.
  - Mitigation: Updated the shared helpers/call sites across gateway, envelope, heartbeat, memory flush, post-compaction context, and system events; added targeted tests.
- Risk: Rejecting invalid timezone strings at schema level could break previously accepted but incorrect configs.
  - Mitigation: This is intentional fail-fast validation; docs and tests cover the accepted IANA-only behavior.

## AI Assistance

This PR was AI-assisted using GPT-5.4 and Gemini 3.1 Pro.

Reject creation of Feishu/Lark announce cron jobs when `delivery.to` is missing.

Delivering to Feishu requires target <chatId|user:openId|chat:chatId>

- add tests covering:
  - Feishu without target → rejected
  - Lark without target → rejected
  - Feishu with explicit target → accepted

## Why both layers

Gateway-side validation is the real safety boundary and prevents invalid jobs from entering the system through any creation path.

CLI-side validation improves user feedback and fails earlier with a clearer message.

## Validation

target.title: fix: guard llm_output hook against undefined lastAssistant after timeout
target.body:
## Summary

- When an LLM request times out before producing any assistant output, `lastAssistant` is `undefined`. The `llm_output` hook was still fired in this case, causing plugin handlers to crash with "Cannot convert undefined or null to object" when they try to enumerate properties of the undefined value.
- Added a null guard: the `llm_output` hook is now only called when `lastAssistant` is defined (i.e., the LLM actually produced output). Also added a `?? undefined` fallback for `getUsageTotals()` to ensure the usage field is cleanly `undefined` rather than potentially `null`.

## Test plan

- [ ] Verify timeout scenarios no longer crash the hook handler
- [ ] Verify normal (non-timeout) LLM responses still trigger the `llm_output` hook as before
- [ ] `pnpm tsgo` passes
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: docs: Clarify Main Session Cron behavior and HEARTBEAT.md integration
target.body:
## Problem

When using Main Session Cron jobs (`--session main --system-event`), the cron event prompt does not include the "Read HEARTBEAT.md" instruction that the default heartbeat prompt contains.

This causes confusion for users who expect their HEARTBEAT.md tasks to be executed when cron events trigger.

**Related Issues:**
- #11726 - Cron jobs with sessionTarget: "main" inject systemEvent but dont trigger agent turn
- #10538 - Cron main-session jobs frequently "skipped: timeout waiting for main lane to become idle"

## Root Cause

In `/app/src/infra/heartbeat-runner.ts`, when cron events are present, the system calls `buildCronEventPrompt()` instead of the default `HEARTBEAT_PROMPT`:

```typescript
const prompt = hasExecCompletion
  ? buildExecEventPrompt({ deliverToUser: params.canRelayToUser })
  : hasCronEvents
    ? buildCronEventPrompt(cronEvents, { deliverToUser: params.canRelayToUser })  // ← No "Read HEARTBEAT.md"
    : resolveHeartbeatPrompt(params.cfg, params.heartbeat);  // ← Contains "Read HEARTBEAT.md"
```

## Changes

1. **`/app/src/infra/heartbeat-events-filter.ts`** - Added code comment explaining the behavior and workaround
2. **`/app/docs/automation/cron-jobs.md`** - Added "HEARTBEAT.md Integration" section with workaround guidance
3. **`/app/docs/reference/templates/AGENTS.md`** - Added "Handling Cron System Events" section to template

## Workaround for Users

Until this is fixed, users can add the following to their `AGENTS.md`:

```markdown
## Cron System Event Handling

When you receive a cron system event (prompt contains "A scheduled reminder has been triggered"):
1. Read HEARTBEAT.md to find the task configuration for this event
2. Execute the task (e.g., send daily brief, wake-up call, etc.)
3. Report completion to the user
```

## Future Work

Consider adding a config option to include HEARTBEAT.md instruction in cron event prompts, or merge cron event handling with heartbeat prompt logic for consistency.

target.title: feat(feishu): comprehensive plugin enhancements — streaming, dedup, skills, calendar, and stability
target.body:
## Summary

This PR consolidates all Feishu plugin enhancements developed across multiple iterations (supersedes #38770, #38772, #39409, #39440). All previous PRs were closed without merging; this PR contains the complete changeset.

---

## Issues Fixed

### Streaming Card Display

1. **Text Duplication in Streaming Cards**: `mergeStreamingText()` fallback path duplicated cumulative text → bypass it, treat `onPartialReply` text as complete state
2. **Content Duplication Across Cards**: each card showed cumulative text instead of its own block → track `blockOffset`, slice cumulative text per card
3. **Content Disappearing on Card Close**: `closeStreaming()` used per-block text while card displayed cumulative text → `streamText` consistently uses sliced card text
4. **Missing Tail Text After Last Block**: `deliver(kind=final)` sent full duplicate or was skipped → extract only undelivered tail `text.slice(blockOffset)`, send if non-empty
5. **Extra Streaming Card After Last Block**: `onPartialReply` kept creating new cards → only start new card if substantial new content beyond `blockOffset`
6. **Slow Streaming Updates**: `await this.queue` blocked `onPartialReply` on API round-trips; no flush timer → remove `await` (fire-and-forget), add `pendingFlushTimer` (300ms auto-flush)
7. **Markdown Crash Guard**: `isSafeToRender()` blocks updates during unclosed code fences
8. **Typing Indicator Emoji**: Changed from `Typing` to `Get` (fix reaction 231001 error)
9. **Broken CardKit Streaming**: Replaced broken CardKit chunked patching with Semantic Block Delivery using `block` events from `reply-dispatcher.ts`

### Message Deduplication (3-Layer)

10. **event_id Dedup**: Lark SDK `EventDispatcher.parse()` flattens header into callback data — `event_id` IS available. Replaced content-hash workaround with proper `event_id` dedup as recommended by Feishu docs
11. **Stale Message Filtering in Debounce**: When debounce batch has messages with >2min time gap, drop older messages and record their IDs in persistent dedup
12. **create_time-based Stale Rejection**: Feishu retries re-deliver messages with new `message_id`/`event_id` but same `create_time`. Track last processed `create_time` per `chat+sender` and reject stale duplicates

### Message Control & Abort

13. **Fast Abort Bypass**: Trigger words (`stop`, `停止`, `取消`, `esc`, etc.) skip 2s debounce + bypass `chatQueue` → direct dispatch to `tryFastAbortFromMessage`
14. **Message Recall Abort**: Register `im.message.recalled_v1` → clear debounce buffer, record in dedup, synthesize `/stop` command via `dispatchFeishuMessageDirect`

### Feishu Ops Tools (Skills)

15. **feishu_contacts**: Search contacts with auto-sync from API
16. **feishu_groups**: List/search bot groups with auto-sync
17. **feishu_send**: Send text/file/@all to users or groups
18. **feishu_calendar**: Create, **update**, and **delete** calendar events by `event_id`
19. **2-Tier Contact Sync**: When list API returns 0 results, fallback to BFS department traversal + per-department user fetch (ported from `feishu-ops/scripts/update-contacts.py`)
20. **SKILL.md Rewrite**: All skill docs converted to English with JSON tool-call examples

### Code Quality

21. **Error Messages Standardized to English** across all new modules
22. **Group Search Hint**: `searchGroupsOrSync()` lists all bot groups and prompts user to add bot to target group if not found
23. **Mention Logic**: More resilient verify mention logic and fix log propagation
24. **SDK v1.30+ Response Parsing**: Fix `proactive-send.ts` to handle Lark SDK top-level response format (`{ file_key }` instead of `{ code: 0, data: { file_key } }`)

### Other

25. **Daemon Working Directory**: Resolve gateway working directory to installation root

---

## Files Changed

| File | Changes |
|---|---|
| `extensions/feishu/src/bot.ts` | event_id dedup, create_time dedup, fast abort bypass, recall handler, mention logic |
| `extensions/feishu/src/dedup.ts` | `checkAndRecordCreateTime()`, create_time tracking per chat+sender |
| `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts` | Block-boundary card splitting, blockOffset tracking, tail text, semantic block delivery |
| `extensions/feishu/src/streaming-card.ts` | Remove mergeStreamingText, async queue, pendingFlushTimer, isSafeToRender |
| `extensions/feishu/src/typing.ts` | Change typing emoji to Get |
| `extensions/feishu/src/inbound-debounce.ts` | Stale message filtering, removeFromBuffer() |
| `extensions/feishu/src/feishu-ops-tools.ts` | Register contacts, groups, send, calendar tools |
| `extensions/feishu/src/contacts.ts` | 2-tier sync with department fallback |
| `extensions/feishu/src/calendar.ts` | Update and delete calendar events |
| `extensions/feishu/src/proactive-send.ts` | SDK v1.30+ response parsing fix |
| `extensions/feishu/skills/*/SKILL.md` | English rewrite with JSON examples |
| `src/daemon/program-args.ts` | Working directory resolution fix |

## Supersedes

- #38770 (CLOSED — streaming card fix)
- #38772 (CLOSED — streaming + event_id dedup)
- #39409 (CLOSED — message control, skills, stability)

## Summary

Implement Feishu channel health-signal reporting so gateway health monitoring can observe runtime activity correctly:

- add a status sink path from Feishu channel startup into monitor runtime updates
- report lifecycle connectivity (`connected`) and mode (`websocket`/`webhook`)
- report `lastEventAt` on inbound Feishu events
- clear connectivity on shutdown/error paths

This improves health monitor observability for Feishu channels and reduces false unhealthy restarts caused by missing runtime activity signals.

## Changes

- `extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts`
  - pass `statusSink` into `monitorFeishuProvider` (`ctx.setStatus` patch sink)
- `extensions/feishu/src/monitor.ts`
  - plumb `statusSink` through monitor options to single-account monitors
- `extensions/feishu/src/monitor.account.ts`
  - accept `statusSink`
  - mark inbound events as activity (`lastEventAt`) and connected
  - initialize mode/connected status for account monitor lifecycle
- `extensions/feishu/src/monitor.transport.ts`
  - accept `statusSink`
  - websocket/webhook start/abort/error paths update connectivity/error status
  - webhook request ingress updates `lastEventAt`

## Issue

target.title: fix(routing): map discord account IDs to matching agents
target.body:
## Summary
- add Discord-only fallback routing: when no binding matches, map `accountId` to an agent with the same ID
- keep fallback disabled for the `default` account id to avoid surprising overrides
- add regression tests for positive auto-match and default-account guard

## Why
In multi-account Discord setups, account names are commonly aligned with agent IDs. Without explicit bindings, traffic incorrectly fell back to the default agent, which could bypass intended per-agent permission boundaries.

## Behavior
When `channel=discord` and no explicit binding matches:
- if `accountId` matches an existing agent id (case-insensitive), route to that agent
- if `accountId=default`, keep existing default-agent behavior

## Test evidence

target.title: fix(spawn): allow mode="session" without thread=true for headless persistent sessions
target.body:
## Summary

- Removes the guard that blocked `mode="session"` when `thread=true` was not set in both `sessions_spawn` (subagent) and ACP spawn paths
- Enables headless persistent sub-agent sessions on **all channels** (Telegram, Slack, WhatsApp, Signal, etc.), not just Discord
- Thread binding remains optional — when `thread=true` is passed, it still attempts platform thread binding as before

## Problem

`sessions_spawn` with `mode: "session"` was hardcoded to require `thread: true`. Since thread binding currently only works on Discord (via `subagent_spawning` hooks), persistent sub-agent sessions were completely unavailable on all other channels. This blocked the orchestrator pattern (main → orchestrator → workers) documented in the official docs from working on non-Discord channels.

## Fix

The guard clause `if (spawnMode === "session" && !requestThreadBinding)` is removed from both `subagent-spawn.ts` and `acp-spawn.ts`. Session persistence and thread binding are independent concepts:

1. **Session persistence** (`mode: "session"`) — session stays alive to receive announces and `sessions_send`
2. **Thread binding** (`thread: true`) — routes platform thread I/O to the session (Discord/Slack feature)

The rest of the code already handles the `thread=false` case correctly — thread binding is only attempted `if (requestThreadBinding)` at line 449.

Fixes #23414

## Test plan

- [x] Updated existing test to verify `mode="session"` without `thread=true` proceeds without error
- [x] All 9 spawn hooks tests pass
- [ ] Manual: spawn sub-agent with `mode: "session"` on Telegram — should succeed
- [ ] Manual: spawn sub-agent with `mode: "session", thread: true` on Discord — should still work as before

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

target.title: fix(agents): normalize mangled tool names and IDs from OpenAI-compati…
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** Some OpenAI-compatible providers (e.g. Kimi) send tool call IDs like `functions.exec:0` as `functions exec:0` (space instead of dot) and tool names like `exec` as `functions exec`. Tool result matching fails, causing "Tool functions exec not found" errors.
- **Why it matters:** Users on Kimi and similar providers cannot complete tool-using flows; matching is done by ID and name, and the mangled forms did not match.
- **What changed:** Added `normalizeMangledToolCallId()` in `tool-call-id.ts` to rewrite `functions ` -> `functions.` in IDs; use it in `extractToolResultId()` and as the key in `sanitizeToolCallIdsForCloudCodeAssist()` so mangled and canonical IDs map to the same sanitized value. Added `normalizePrefixedToolName()` in `session-transcript-repair.ts` to strip `functions.` / `functions ` from tool names; applied in assistant block handling, `hasToolCallName`, and `normalizeToolResultName()` so tool-result names are normalized and pairing works.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No change to provider clients or wire format; only transcript repair and ID sanitization for matching.

- [x] Bug fix

3. After fix: IDs and names normalize; pairing and sanitization map mangled and canonical forms to the same value.

Tool results pair with assistant tool calls; no spurious "tool not found" from ID/name mangling.

As expected after the fix.

target.title: feat(exec): add bubblewrap namespace sandbox for safeBins commands
target.body:
## What

Adds kernel-level filesystem isolation for safeBins-approved commands using [bubblewrap](https://github.com/containers/bubblewrap) (bwrap) unprivileged user namespaces on Linux.

## Why

The current safeBins fence validates command names but can't prevent absolute-path bypasses (e.g. `/usr/bin/rm`). With bwrap, the binary simply doesn't exist inside the sandbox — filesystem isolation is enforced by the kernel, not by argument parsing.

## How

When `tools.exec.nsSandbox.mode` is set to `"bwrap"`, commands that pass safeBins validation are wrapped in a bwrap namespace where only:

- **Approved binaries** — resolved from `trustedSafeBinDirs`, mounted read-only
- **Shell binaries** — `sh`, `bash`, `env` always available for `sh -c`
- **System libraries** — `/lib`, `/usr/lib` etc. for dynamic linking (read-only)
- **System config** — SSL certs, DNS, locale (read-only)
- **Working directory** — mounted read-write
- **Extra mounts** — configurable via `nsSandbox.extraBinds`

Everything else is invisible. Namespace isolation uses `--unshare-all --share-net --die-with-parent`.

### Interaction with other security layers

| State | Behavior |
|---|---|
| Trust window active | No sandbox (run directly) |
| No trust + safeBins match + bwrap enabled | Wrapped in bwrap namespace |
| No trust + safeBins match + bwrap disabled | Run directly (existing behavior) |
| No trust + not safeBins | Ask for approval (existing behavior) |
| Docker sandbox (`host=sandbox`) | Docker takes precedence, no bwrap |
| PTY requested | Skip bwrap (terminal allocation unreliable in namespaces) |

Composes cleanly with trust windows (PR #39126).

### Config

```jsonc
{
  "tools": {
    "exec": {
      "nsSandbox": {
        "mode": "bwrap",  // "bwrap" | "none" (default)
        "extraBinds": [
          { "src": "/opt/data", "writable": false },
          { "src": "/opt/output", "writable": true }
        ]
      }
    }
  }
}
```

Requires bubblewrap installed (`bwrap`). Ships with Fedora/Ubuntu via Flatpak deps. Falls back to no-sandbox gracefully if not available.

## Files changed (8)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `src/infra/exec-bwrap-sandbox.ts` | **New** — Core module: bwrap arg builder, detection, config normalization |
| `src/infra/exec-bwrap-sandbox.test.ts` | **New** — 18 unit tests |
| `src/config/types.tools.ts` | Add `nsSandbox` to `ExecToolConfig` |
| `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-types.ts` | Add `nsSandbox` to `ExecToolDefaults` |
| `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts` | Add bwrap spawn path in `runExecProcess` |
| `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-host-gateway.ts` | Build bwrap params when safeBins match |
| `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts` | Thread bwrap params from gateway → runtime |
| `src/agents/pi-tools.ts` | Thread `nsSandbox` config through defaults |

## Testing

- 18 new unit tests for bwrap module (arg building, mount dedup, config normalization, binary resolution)
- 47 existing safeBins tests still passing
- `tsc --noEmit` — zero errors from our changes
- Manual: bwrap v0.11.0 on Fedora 43

## AI Disclosure

AI-assisted (Tommy/Claude Opus 4.6). All code reviewed and understood by contributor.

target.title: feat(agents): add opt-in CaMeL prompt injection defense
target.body:
## Summary

Adds opt-in CaMeL (CApabilities for MachinE Learning) prompt injection defense to OpenClaw. Native TypeScript implementation inspired by Google DeepMind's [CaMeL paper](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.18813).

**Why:** Prompt injection is the #1 OWASP vulnerability for LLM apps. Detection-based defenses fail under adaptive attack ([100% bypass rate](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.15511)). CaMeL takes a structural approach: instead of detecting injections, it makes them unable to influence program flow — like parameterized queries solved SQL injection.

**What it does:** Tracks data provenance through tool calls. Untrusted data (web content, file reads) gets tainted. Tainted values are blocked from reaching side-effect tools (exec, messaging, gateway) unless policy explicitly allows it or the user approves.

Refs: #39160

---

## Architecture

Three isolated LLM sessions, no cross-contamination:

| Session | Role | Has Tools? | Sees System Prompt? |
|---------|------|-----------|-------------------|
| **P-LLM** (Planner) | Generates structured JSON execution plan from user prompt | No | No (isolated) |
| **Q-LLM** (Quarantined) | Extracts values from untrusted data | No | Minimal extraction-only prompt |
| **Main Agent** | Executes approved tool calls, renders results | Yes | Yes |

## Key Design Decisions

- **`CaMeLValue<T>` with Symbol brand** — generic taint wrapper; Symbol prevents spoofed values from bypassing checks
- **Default-deny on side-effect tools** — in strict mode, ALL tainted side-effect tools route through the approval flow (ask the human, not hard deny)
- **Session-level taint tracking** — once any taint source tool runs, ALL subsequent side-effect tool args are marked tainted (LLMs can paraphrase tainted content, defeating substring matching)
- **Coordinated dual enforcement** — orchestrator path and before-tool-call hook don't double-check; active-scopes registry prevents conflicts
- **Gateway approval integration** — CaMeL approvals route through `exec.approval.request` with `source: "camel"` to bypass forwarding config, delivering approval prompts with sanitized content previews to the user's messaging surface (Signal, Discord, Telegram, CLI)
- **Active scope protection** — taint state eviction skips scopes with running orchestrators

## Configuration

```json
{
  "agents": {
    "defaults": {
      "camel": {
        "enabled": false,
        "mode": "strict",
        "qModel": "anthropic/claude-haiku-4",
        "policies": {
          "trustedRecipients": [],

          "noSideEffectTools": ["read", "web_search", "memory_search", "session_status"]
        }
      }
    }
  }
}
```

`enabled: false` by default. Opt-in per agent or globally.

## Files Changed (21 files, +982/-112)

**New CaMeL engine** (`src/agents/camel/`):
- `types.ts`, `value.ts`, `capabilities.ts` — core value system with taint tracking
- `security-policy.ts`, `taint-tracker.ts` — policy engine and taint propagation
- `plan-generator.ts`, `orchestrator.ts` — P-LLM plan generation and execution
- `quarantined-llm.ts` — Q-LLM isolation
- `tool-classifications.ts` — side-effect vs read-only tool registry
- `approval-flow.ts` — user consent integration
- `config.ts`, `active-scopes.ts`, `utils.ts` — configuration, scope coordination, shared utils
- `index.ts` — public API

**Integration** (`src/agents/`):
- `pi-tools.before-tool-call.ts` — hook-level taint tracking and policy enforcement
- `pi-embedded-runner/run/attempt.ts` — CaMeL orchestrator integration with isolated P-LLM/Q-LLM sessions
- `tool-policy-pipeline.ts`, `pi-embedded-runner/run.ts`, `run/params.ts` — plumbing

**Config schema** (`src/config/`):
- `types.agents.ts`, `zod-schema.agent-runtime.ts`, `zod-schema.agents.ts`

**Documentation**: `SECURITY.md`

## Tests (28 passing)

- 9 test files covering all CaMeL modules
- Red-team scenarios: web-fetch injection → message exfil, chained tool pivots, exec injection
- Anti-spoofing: Symbol-branded values reject duck-typed fakes
- Structured extraction: JSON objects serialize correctly (not `[object Object]`)
- Policy enforcement: default-deny, trusted recipients, wildcard patterns
- Taint propagation: dependency chains, bidirectional matching

## What This Does NOT Cover (Phase 2)

- Policy configuration UI
- Per-channel approval UX optimization
- Performance profiling
- AgentDojo benchmark integration
- Q-LLM model selection UI

## References

- [Motivation Thread (@RogueEngineer)](https://x.com/RogueEngineer/status/2030485632183685382)

- [CaMeL Paper (arXiv:2503.18813)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2503.18813)
- [Simon Willison's Analysis](https://simonwillison.net/2025/Apr/11/camel/)
- [The Attacker Moves Second (arXiv:2410.15511)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2410.15511)
- [RFC Discussion: #39160](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/39160)

target.title: fix: billing misclassification and duplicate reply for multi-handle users
target.body:
## Summary

Two bug fixes:

### 1. Anthropic "usage limits" errors misclassified as rate_limit

Anthropic returns "You have reached your specified API usage limits. You will regain access on ..." when billing limits are hit. This matches the rateLimit pattern "usage limit" and gets classified as rate_limit instead of billing, causing the failover system to retry (with backoff) instead of immediately skipping to the next provider.

**Fix:**
- Add billing patterns for Anthropic usage limit messages to failover-matches.ts
- Move isBillingErrorMessage check before isRateLimitErrorMessage in both classifyFailoverReason and formatAssistantErrorText

### 2. Duplicate final reply when messaging tool target != originating target

When the agent uses the message tool to send a reply, and the user has multiple identifiers (e.g. email and phone number), the target comparison in shouldSuppressMessagingToolReplies fails because the identifiers don't match. This causes dedupeMessagingToolPayloads to be false, skipping text-based dedup entirely, and the final reply is sent a second time.

**Fix:** Always allow text-based dedup when messagingToolSentTexts is non-empty. filterMessagingToolDuplicates only removes payloads whose text matches what was already sent, so cross-channel sends with different text are unaffected.

## Test plan

- [x] Existing billing test updated with Anthropic "usage limits" pattern
- [x] Existing classifyFailoverReason test updated to verify billing classification
- [ ] Verify no regressions in unit test suite
- [ ] Manual test: send message via iMessage from phone number, verify no duplicate reply

target.title: fix: strip mcp_ prefix in executeToolCalls tool name lookup
target.body:
Summary

•  Problem: executeToolCalls() in @mariozechner/pi-agent-core did exact name matching — when the LLM called mcp_exec, it couldn't find the tool registered as exec, throwing Tool mcp_exec not found.
•  Why it matters: Any session where the LLM generated mcp_-prefixed tool calls silently errored out, breaking exec/read/write operations mid-turn with no graceful fallback.
•  What changed: Added a pnpm patch to @mariozechner/pi-agent-core@0.55.3 — one line before the tools.find() call that strips a leading mcp_ prefix from the tool name before lookup.
•  What did NOT change: Tool registration names, system prompt tool lists, routing logic, and all other call sites are untouched. The normalization is purely at dispatch time.

Change Type

•  [v] Bug fix

Scope

•  [v] Skills / tool execution

Linked Issue/PR

•  Closes #
•  Related #

User-visible / Behavior Changes

Tool calls with mcp_ prefix (e.g. mcp_exec, mcp_read, mcp_write) now execute correctly instead of returning an error. Previously these silently failed and the agent would report the error back to the user or retry.

Security Impact

•  Command/tool execution surface changed? Yes — previously, mcp_exec was effectively a no-op (errored). It now correctly executes. This restores intended behavior; no new surface is opened since these tools were already registered and reachable without the prefix.

Repro + Verification

Environment

•  OS: macOS
•  Runtime/container: macOS, Node 25.8.0
•  Model/provider: anthropic/claude-opus-4-6
•  Integration/channel: Feishu
•  Relevant config: tools.profile = "full", sandbox.mode = "off"

Steps

1. Send a message that causes the LLM to call mcp_exec (e.g. "Use mcp_exec to run: echo hello")
2. Observe session logs — look for toolResult with toolName: "mcp_exec"
3. Without patch: response contains Tool mcp_exec not found. With patch: command executes and returns output.

Expected

•  Agent executes the command and returns stdout.

Actual (before fix)

•  Agent returns Tool mcp_exec not found error in tool result.

Evidence

•  [v] Trace/log snippets — session 79c343f1: L20 toolResult shows toolName: "mcp_exec" with Tool mcp_exec not found before fix.
•  [v] Passing after — 5 consecutive fresh-session calls to mcp_exec all returned correct output after patch applied.

Human Verification

•  Verified scenarios: 5 consecutive openclaw agent calls with mcp_exec, mcp_read, mcp_write across fresh sessions — all succeeded.
•  Edge cases checked: Tool called without prefix (exec) still works; mcp_ prefix on non-existent tool still throws Tool not found as expected.
•  What you did not verify: Behavior on other LLM providers (OpenAI, Gemini); behavior when tool name is exactly mcp_ with no suffix.

Compatibility / Migration

•  Backward compatible? Yes — calls without mcp_ prefix are unchanged.
•  Config/env changes? No
•  Migration needed? No

Failure Recovery

•  How to revert: Remove patches/@mariozechner__pi-agent-core@0.55.3.patch and the patchedDependencies entry from package.json, then pnpm install.
•  Files to restore: package.json (remove pnpm.patchedDependencies), delete patches/@mariozechner__pi-agent-core@0.55.3.patch.
•  Bad symptoms to watch for: If the patch fails to apply on pnpm install, the build will error — check pnpm install output for patch rejection.

Risks and Mitigations

•  Risk: A tool legitimately named mcp_something (if one is ever registered) would incorrectly resolve to something.
◦  Mitigation: OpenClaw's canonical tool registry has no mcp_-prefixed names and the naming convention explicitly forbids them. The prefix is only an LLM artifact. If upstream ever ships an mcp_-prefixed tool name, this patch should be revisited.

target.title: fix(pre-commit): use single pytest dependency specifier
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** The pre-commit config had `additional_dependencies: [pytest>=8, <9]`. In YAML this is parsed as two list items (`pytest>=8` and `<9`), so pip tries to install a package named `<9` and fails with `Invalid requirement: '<9': Expected package name at the start of dependency specifier`.
- **Why it matters:** Pre-commit environment setup fails for anyone running hooks; the skills-python-tests hook cannot install.
- **What changed:** Single specifier as one string: `["pytest>=8,<9"]` so pip receives a valid PEP 440 range.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary):** No other files; no voice-call or other plugin changes. (Schema fixes are in #38892.)

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [x] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Related #39109 (closed as duplicate for voice-call; this PR is the pre-commit-only fix from that contribution)
- Related #

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

**None.** Pre-commit hook env install succeeds; no runtime behavior change.

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? **No**
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? **No**
- New/changed network calls? **No**
- Command/tool execution surface changed? **No**
- Data access scope changed? **No**

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any
- pre-commit with the skills-python-tests hook

### Steps

1. Run `pre-commit run --all-files` or trigger a commit that runs hooks.
2. Before: install step fails with invalid requirement `<9`.
3. After: dependency installs; hook can run (or skip if no skills/*.py changed).

### Expected

Pre-commit installs the local hook env without error.

### Actual

As expected after the fix.

## Evidence

- [x] Single-line YAML fix; pip accepts `pytest>=8,<9` as one specifier.
- [ ] Trace/log snippets: N/A
- [ ] Screenshot/recording: N/A

## Human Verification (required)

- **Verified scenarios:** Changed to `["pytest>=8,<9"]`; confirmed YAML list has one element and pip install succeeds.
- **Edge cases checked:** N/A.
- **What you did not verify:** Full pre-commit matrix on all platforms.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? **Yes**
- Config/env changes? **No**
- Migration needed? **No**

## Failure Recovery (if this breaks)

- Revert the single line in `.pre-commit-config.yaml`.
- No config to restore.

## Risks and Mitigations

**None.** One-string dependency spec; no behavior change beyond fixing install.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: The `skills-python-tests` pre-commit hook now carries one valid pytest dependency string instead of a YAML list that splits into `pytest>=8` and an invalid standalone `<9`.

Real environment tested: Local checkout of the PR branch in a real terminal session.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
sed -n '104,116p' .pre-commit-config.yaml
ruby -e 'require "yaml"; data = YAML.load_file(".pre-commit-config.yaml"); hook = data["repos"].find { |r| r["repo"] == "local" }["hooks"].find { |h| h["id"] == "skills-python-tests" }; p hook["additional_dependencies"]'
```

Evidence after fix:
```text
$ sed -n '104,116p' .pre-commit-config.yaml
  - repo: local
    hooks:
      - id: skills-python-tests
        name: skills python tests
        entry: pytest -q skills
        language: python
        additional_dependencies: ["pytest>=8,<9"]
        pass_filenames: false
        files: "^skills/.*\\.py$"
```

```text
$ ruby -e 'require "yaml"; data = YAML.load_file(".pre-commit-config.yaml"); hook = data["repos"].find { |r| r["repo"] == "local" }["hooks"].find { |h| h["id"] == "skills-python-tests" }; p hook["additional_dependencies"]'
["pytest>=8,<9"]
```

Observed result after fix: The hook definition on this branch loads as a single dependency entry, so pre-commit no longer forwards a bogus standalone `<9` requirement to pip.

What was not tested: A full end-to-end `pre-commit run --all-files` install in a fresh environment on every platform.

target.title: feat(agents): per-agent outbound A2A allowlist override
target.body:
## Summary

- Add `agents.list[].tools.agentToAgent.allow` to control which agents a specific agent may contact via session tools (`sessions_send`, `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, `session_status`)
- When unset, the agent falls back to the global `tools.agentToAgent.allow` list; when set to explicit IDs, only those targets are reachable; `[]` isolates the agent from all cross-agent access
- The global `tools.agentToAgent.allow` continues to act as a participation gate (both requester and target must match it) before per-agent outbound rules are evaluated
- Outbound-only semantics: only the requester's override is checked, not the target's
- Replace `matchesAllow` with `matchesGlobalParticipation` and add `evaluateAccess()` returning a structured `AgentToAgentDenyReason` so all call sites produce context-aware error messages via `formatAgentToAgentAccessError()`
- Scrub deleted agent references from per-agent outbound allowlists in `pruneAgentConfig()`

### Example config (hub-spoke pattern)

```json
{
  "tools": {
    "agentToAgent": { "enabled": true, "allow": ["*"] }
  },
  "agents": {
    "list": [
      { "id": "david",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["*"] } } },
      { "id": "spoke",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["david"] } } },
      { "id": "kelly",  "tools": { "agentToAgent": { "allow": ["david"] } } }
    ]
  }
}
```

spoke→david ✅ | spoke→kelly ❌ | david→anyone ✅

## Test plan

- [x] `sessions-access.test.ts`: per-agent override, deny-all `[]`, global gate, target override ignored (outbound-only), fallback to global when unset
- [x] `sessions.test.ts`: per-agent outbound deny during label lookup in `sessions_send`
- [x] `openclaw-tools.session-status.test.ts`: per-agent outbound deny for cross-agent `session_status`
- [x] `agents.test.ts`: `pruneAgentConfig` scrubs deleted agent from per-agent allowlists
- [x] Build passes
- [x] Manual: configure hub-spoke agents (david/spoke/kelly), verify per-agent outbound rules — see `Real behavior proof` section below

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: per-agent outbound `agentToAgent.allow` correctly gates cross-agent session tool access. Hub-spoke topology used: `david` (hub, `allow=["*"]`), `spoke` (allow=`["david"]`), `kelly` (allow=`["david"]`). Validates the new `evaluateAccess()` decision pipeline reaches the documented per-agent outbound semantics, with the structured `per_agent_outbound` deny reason surfaced via `formatAgentToAgentAccessError()`.

- **Real environment tested**: Ubuntu 22.04 (kernel 5.15), Node 22.22.0, pnpm 10. Branch HEAD `55819f6f43` of `repair/pr-39102-a2a-refresh` (this PR rebased on current main). Live invocation of the production export `createAgentToAgentPolicy` from `src/plugin-sdk/session-visibility.ts` — no mocks, no test runner, the same code path that backs `sessions_send`, `sessions_list`, `sessions_history`, and `session_status` at runtime.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

  1. Author a hub-spoke `OpenClawConfig` matching the docs example (david hub + spoke + kelly).
  2. Build `policy = createAgentToAgentPolicy(cfg)`.
  3. Call `policy.evaluateAccess(requester, target)` for the five edge pairs.
  4. Run with: `pnpm tsx proof-hub-spoke.ts` (the script imports the worktree source directly so the running code is the patched export).

- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output, copied verbatim from `node` stdout via `tsx`.

  ```
  # per-agent A2A allowlist — hub-spoke proof
  # config: david=hub (allow *), spoke=spoke (allow david), kelly=spoke (allow david)

  [ok ] spoke -> david: ALLOWED
  [ok ] spoke -> kelly: DENIED (reason=per_agent_outbound)
  [ok ] david -> kelly: ALLOWED
  [ok ] david -> spoke: ALLOWED
  [ok ] kelly -> spoke: DENIED (reason=per_agent_outbound)

  all cases match expected behavior
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: all five real invocations matched the documented hub-spoke semantics. Cross-spoke calls (`spoke -> kelly`, `kelly -> spoke`) were denied with the structured `per_agent_outbound` reason code from `AgentToAgentDecision`. Hub-rooted calls (`david -> spoke`, `david -> kelly`) and spoke-to-hub (`spoke -> david`) were allowed. Process exit code: `0`. Console output above is the literal stdout, no post-processing.

- **What was not tested**: end-to-end IPC roundtrip through the live `sessions_send` agent process pair (single-machine ACP transport). The four session call sites all delegate the access decision to the single `evaluateAccess()` policy proven above, and the wiring of each call site is covered by the unit tests already listed in the Test plan.

target.title: Telegram: optimize message pipeline performance
target.body:
## Overview

This PR implements performance optimizations for the Telegram message pipeline.

## What

1. **C1 - Parallelize async operations**: `resolveStickerVisionSupport` and `transcribeFirstAudio` run in parallel
2. **H3 - Fast-path command detection**: Added prefix check before iterating aliases  
3. **H5 - Cache Intl.DateTimeFormat**: Cache formatter by timezone
4. **H1 - Static import**: Audio preflight module
5. **Bug fix**: Unhandled rejection fix

## Results

- `hasControlCommand`: 1.74μs → 0.052μs (33x faster)
- `formatInboundEnvelope`: 70.4μs → 43.2μs (38% faster)
- All 720 telegram tests pass

target.title: docs: add Telegram multi-agent group setup example
target.body:
## What

Adds a dedicated section to the multi-agent routing docs (`docs/concepts/multi-agent.md`) covering how to configure multiple Telegram bot accounts in a single group chat.

## Why

The existing multi-agent docs have a WhatsApp group example but no Telegram equivalent. Setting up multiple Telegram bots in one group has several non-obvious gotchas:

- **Privacy Mode** must be disabled in @BotFather *and* bots must be re-added to the group afterward (Telegram caches the setting per-group)
- `groupAllowFrom` filters by **sender user ID**, not group chat ID — this is a common source of confusion
- Both top-level `channels.telegram.groupPolicy` and per-account `groupPolicy` must allow group messages

These are real pain points I hit while setting this up. The new section includes a complete config example and documents all the gotchas.

## Changes

- Added "Multiple Telegram agents in one group" section with:
  - Full config example (agents, accounts, bindings)
  - Step-by-step setup notes
  - Common pitfalls and their solutions

target.title: fix(update): avoid broad tag fetches for dev updates
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `openclaw update --channel dev` could fail before updating `main` when a release tag on the remote conflicted with an existing local tag.
- Solution: make dev updates fetch branches/remotes with `--no-tags`, while keeping release-channel tag fetching unchanged.
- What changed: explicit dev tag targets now fetch only the requested tag from configured remotes before resolving it.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): stable/beta updates remain tag-driven and still fetch tags broadly.

## Motivation

- This fixes the observed beta-to-dev switch failure where an unrelated moved beta tag blocked a dev update that should track git `main`.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [x] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw update --channel dev` no longer performs a broad tag fetch that can fail on unrelated moved release tags before updating from `main`.
- Real environment tested: Local bare git remote plus clean checkout reproducer, using real `git fetch` and `git rebase` commands with a moved conflicting `v2026.5.19-beta.2` tag.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Created a local bare remote, cloned it, moved the remote beta tag, confirmed current-main-style `git fetch --all --prune --tags` fails, then confirmed PR-style `git fetch --all --prune --no-tags` followed by `git rebase origin/main` succeeds.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

```text
proof_root=<temp-redacted>

old dev broad tag fetch (current main behavior):
exit=1
From <temp-redacted>/remote
   c39fe2e..63b8b7c  main              -> origin/main
 ! [rejected]        v2026.5.19-beta.2 -> v2026.5.19-beta.2  (would clobber existing tag)

reset checkout to before proof fetch:
clean_status=0

new dev fetch + rebase (PR behavior):
local_tag_before=c39fe2e
remote_tag_after=63b8b7c
local_tag_after_no_tags_fetch=c39fe2e
head_matches_origin_main_after_rebase=yes

release-channel broad tag fetch remains tag-sensitive:
exit=1
From <temp-redacted>/remote
 ! [rejected]        v2026.5.19-beta.2 -> v2026.5.19-beta.2  (would clobber existing tag)
```

Supplemental focused test proof:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/update-runner.test.ts
Test Files 1 passed (1)
Tests 46 passed (46)
```

- Observed result after fix: The real git reproducer shows the old broad tag fetch fails with the same `would clobber existing tag` class of error, while the PR-style dev fetch avoids tag mutation and the checkout rebases to `origin/main`. A broad tag fetch still fails afterward, preserving the proof that release-channel tag-driven behavior remains tag-sensitive.
- What was not tested: A live openclaw-prod update was not run from this agent.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): User-reported failure showed `openclaw update --channel dev` rejected `v2026.5.19-beta.2` during broad tag fetch with `would clobber existing tag`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Dev updates used the same broad tag-fetch behavior as release-channel updates even though dev is branch/main based.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Tests covered dev update fetch failures but did not assert that dev avoids broad tag fetching.
- Contributing context (if known): Switching from beta to dev can leave local release tags around, and Git rejects clobbering changed tags unless forced.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [ ] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/infra/update-runner.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: Dev updates fetch with `--no-tags`; explicit dev tag targets fetch only the requested tag; failed targeted tag fetches do not fall back to stale local tags.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: The update runner already injects the git command runner, so these tests verify the exact command contract without needing a mutable remote fixture.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): Existing dev update tests covered fetch sequencing but not the tag-fetch mode.
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

`openclaw update --channel dev` now ignores unrelated remote tags during the normal branch update path. Explicit dev tag targets still fetch the requested tag.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
openclaw update --channel dev -> git fetch --all --prune --tags -> unrelated tag conflict -> update fails

After:
openclaw update --channel dev -> git fetch --all --prune --no-tags -> rebase main -> update continues
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: The git fetch command changes for dev updates only. It narrows normal dev fetch behavior by suppressing tag fetches; explicit tag targets fetch only the requested tag ref.

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: Linux worktree
- Runtime/container: Node via repo test wrapper; local git bare remote proof
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel (if any): update CLI git mode
- Relevant config (redacted): N/A

### Steps

1. Create a local bare git remote and clone it.
2. Create local tag `v2026.5.19-beta.2`, then move the remote tag to a newer commit.
3. Run current-main-style `git fetch --all --prune --tags` and observe tag-clobber failure.
4. Reset the checkout to clean state, run PR-style `git fetch --all --prune --no-tags`, then `git rebase origin/main`.
5. Run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/update-runner.test.ts`.

### Expected

- Current-main-style broad tag fetch fails on the moved tag.
- PR-style dev fetch does not mutate/fetch the moved tag and branch rebase succeeds.
- Stable/beta paths continue to use tag fetching.
- Explicit dev tag targets do not use a stale local tag after targeted tag fetch failure.

### Actual

- Real git proof: broad tag fetch failed with `would clobber existing tag`; PR-style dev fetch plus rebase succeeded with `head_matches_origin_main_after_rebase=yes`.
- `src/infra/update-runner.test.ts` passed 46/46.

## Evidence

Attach at least one:

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

What you personally verified (not just CI), and how:

- Verified scenarios: real local bare-remote moved-tag reproduction, targeted update-runner tests, `git diff --check`, and `codex review --base origin/main` after rebase.
- Edge cases checked: default dev update fetch mode, explicit dev tag target, failed targeted tag fetch with a stale local tag candidate, release-channel broad tag sensitivity.
- What you did **not** verify: live update on openclaw-prod.

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [ ] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes/No`) Yes
- Config/env changes? (`Yes/No`) No
- Migration needed? (`Yes/No`) No
- If yes, exact upgrade steps: N/A

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Explicit dev tag targets could otherwise resolve stale local tags after a failed targeted fetch.
  - Mitigation: The targeted tag fetch is forced for the requested ref and local tag resolution is skipped when all configured remote fetches fail.

target.title: Retry app-server bridge drops safely
target.body:
## Summary
- Retry Codex app-server bridge client-close and turn-completion idle failures once when the run has not produced visible output, persisted the queued user message, sent through the message tool, or started/completed a mutating tool.
- Surface repeated or skipped app-server bridge failures as visible Codex app-server errors so group/channel replies do not silently disappear behind generic runner suppression.
- Mark queued/direct block replies, partial/reasoning/tool-result output, compaction notices, messaging sends, and generic mutating tool side effects as retry blockers, including errored mutating tool results.

## Root Cause
PR #83827 is still present upstream, and this is a different failure signature. The observed drops match Codex app-server lifecycle failures such as `codex app-server client closed before turn completed` and `codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for turn/completed`; those were flowing through the generic before-reply failure path, which can become silent in group/channel conversations.

The safe recovery boundary is narrower than “before final reply”: once any user-visible output, transcript user-message persistence, message-tool send, or mutating tool side effect has happened, replaying the turn can duplicate messages or side effects. The P1 follow-up found that errored mutating tool results were still clearing the pending side-effect latch; this patch now treats completion of a mutating tool call as a replay blocker even when the tool reports an error.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: app-server client-close/turn-completion idle failures before reply no longer disappear silently; clean pre-output failures retry once; post-output/post-side-effect failures do not replay.

Real environment tested: local tmp worktree after rebasing onto current `origin/main`; live OpenClaw Telegram DM production runtime after the fix path was deployed. The exact original production screenshot is attached as provided by the operator.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.test.ts`; `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`; `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`; live production Telegram DM text prompt; live production Telegram DM prompt containing one JPEG screenshot attachment.

Evidence after fix: oxfmt reported all matched files use the correct format; `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD` passed; earlier Vitest proof reported `Test Files 2 passed (2)` and `Tests 131 passed (131)`; the P1 follow-up focused Vitest proof reported `Test Files 1 passed (1)` and `Tests 121 passed (121)`; autoreview reported `autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported`. In live production, a Telegram DM text-only prompt received a delivered OpenClaw reply, and a later screenshot/JPEG prompt also received a delivered OpenClaw reply. The local session record for the live production proof shows Telegram as the source channel, one media-bearing inbound message for the screenshot case, and successful `message` tool results (`ok: true`) for the text and media replies. Original screenshot proof is attached in the PR conversation: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/VACInc/openclaw/pr-84219-production-proof-artifacts/pr-assets/84219/live-production-telegram-proof-redacted.jpg?v=3

Observed result after fix: app-server bridge failures before output retry and can recover; repeated failures produce visible Codex app-server copy in group chats; retries are skipped after block/partial/reasoning/tool-result output, compaction notices, queued user persistence, messaging sends, pending messaging sends, completed mutating tools, errored completed mutating tools, and pending mutating tools. Live production Telegram DM text and screenshot prompts produced visible replies instead of being lost, confirming the issue is fixed in the production path covered by this PR.

What was not tested: full `pnpm check`/changed lanes or broad Testbox proof.

## Verification
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/reply-delivery.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `AUTOREVIEW_AUTO_TESTS=0 .agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- Live production Telegram DM text prompt: delivered OpenClaw reply observed.
- Live production Telegram DM screenshot/JPEG prompt: delivered OpenClaw reply observed.
- Original live production screenshot proof attached in the PR conversation.

## What was not tested
- Full `pnpm check`/changed lanes or Testbox proof.

target.title: fix(memory): stop recall tracking when dreaming is disabled
target.body:
## Summary
- stop normal `memory_search` recall tracking from writing `.dreams` artifacts when `plugins.entries.memory-core.config.dreaming.enabled=false`
- keep ordinary memory search results unchanged while preserving dreaming timezone behavior whenever dreaming is enabled
- add regression coverage for the tool path, CLI path, and positive recall-tracking cases so tests explicitly model dreaming on/off behavior

Closes #84436.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/memory-core/src/tools.recall-tracking.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/tools.citations.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/cli.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: ordinary `memory_search` and `openclaw memory search` were still writing `memory/.dreams/short-term-recall.json` and `memory/.dreams/events.jsonl` even when the top-level `dreaming.enabled` master switch was off.
Real environment tested: isolated local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS using a temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and workspace with bundled `memory-core` active and `plugins.entries.memory-core.config.dreaming.enabled=false`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: create a temp workspace with `memory/2026-05-20.md`, point `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` at the temp config/state, then run `pnpm openclaw memory search glacier --json`, followed by direct filesystem checks for `memory/.dreams/short-term-recall.json` and `memory/.dreams/events.jsonl`.
Evidence after fix: terminal output from the live run:
```text
$ OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-proof-YP473f/openclaw.json OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-proof-YP473f/state pnpm openclaw memory search glacier --json
[memory] chunks_vec not updated — semantic vector embeddings unavailable — no vector dimensions resolved. Vector recall degraded. Further duplicate warnings suppressed.
{
  "results": [
    {
      "path": "memory/2026-05-20.md",
      "startLine": 1,
      "endLine": 3,
      "score": 0.6466676666656668,
      "snippet": "# Daily Memory\n- Move backups to S3 Glacier after the May retention window.\n",
      "source": "memory"
    }
  ]
}
short-term-recall.json: missing
events.jsonl: missing
```
Observed result after fix: the real CLI search still returned the expected memory hit from `memory/2026-05-20.md`, and the follow-up filesystem checks confirmed that neither `memory/.dreams/short-term-recall.json` nor `memory/.dreams/events.jsonl` was created.
What was not tested: packaged/Testbox/Crabbox proof on Windows; this proof covers a real local OpenClaw CLI run in an isolated source-checkout setup.
